_comment: Content ladder for the autonomous content runs. Each run builds the first entry whose status is 'todo', wires it to be reachable in play, passes the self-verify gate (scripts/selfplay.mjs + size-limit), then flips it to 'done'. Never repeat a done entry.

ladder.summary: Populate lj.enemy.types.blue {a,b,c,d,B} (frost/arcane theme) and add lj.enemy.familyForRealm(size) so realm 2+ spawns the blue family. Reuses existing a/b/c/d/B tile symbols (single-row sprite sheet has no per-color art).

Populate lj.enemy.types.green {a,b,c,d,B} ("The Blightwood" poison/nature theme) and extend the familyForRealm ladder so realm 3+ spawns green. familyLadder auto-picks up the populated family; realm 3=green, realm 4+ clamp to green. Step over blue kept tiny (<=1.17x agility/HP, armor untouched) because the realm-3+ clamp compounds hard with 1.4^(size-1) scaling.

Populate lj.enemy.types.red {a,b,c,d,B} ("The Emberdeep" molten/infernal theme) so the familyLadder auto-routes realm 4+ to red. Reuses a/b/c/d/B tile symbols (already in spriteMap + creaturesAndItemsMap). Red now owns realm 4+ — which selfplay shows is essentially the whole decisive game (nearly every win/death) — so green retreats to realm 3 alone and the green->red step is deliberately gentle: armor UNTOUCHED on every grade (it is a % dmg-reduction that compounds hardest with depth), escalation is +1 HP/grade plus +1 agility on striker+boss and +1 boss crit. A full notch (flat +1 str on striker+boss) cratered the sim to ~24% (below the 25% floor); this holds ~28-29%, a clean ~4pt step below green mirroring blue->green.

Added The Cinderwyrm (red grade "T"), the first boss with a real combat mechanic: ENRAGE. lj.enemy.monster carries an optional mechanic descriptor; lj.enemy.base copies it onto instances; lj.enemy.enraged(enemy,curHp,maxHp) returns a boosted attack snapshot below threshold*maxHp and the fight loop (lj.hero.fight in combat.js) resolves the enemy's swing through it, so damage ramps mid-fight. Reachable: lj.enemy.bossGradeForRealm(size) -> "T" at realm 5+ (else "B"), placed in realm.js, routed in hero.js (creaturesAndItemsMap + lj.enemy.isBoss level-up), rendered via scene.js spriteMap (reuses boss sprite [0,9]); selfplay fights the per-realm boss grade. Tuned as a risk/reward trade: base offense [5,7] gentler than the Emberlord's [7,8], enrage (0.4 threshold, 1.5x str&agi) makes the sub-40% back half hit ~[8,11] — harder than the old boss. Net ~29.3% win rate (A/B: enrage off 33.5% vs on 28.4%, ~5pt swing => load-bearing).

Added the 'Spoils of the Realms' loot affix batch to js/stats.js: 4 prefixes (Frostforged, Venomous, Emberforged, Wyrmscaled) + 4 suffixes (of the Frostmarch, of the Blightwood, of the Emberdeep, of the Cinderwyrm), one prefix+suffix pair per enemy family (blue/green/red/apex) so the loot pool mirrors the tiers the hero fights. Auto-reachable: lj.items.makeItem picks rng(0,len-1) across the full prefixes/suffixes arrays and applies each ~30% per item, so pushing onto the arrays is the only wiring needed; affixes never render as map tiles, so scene.js spriteMap / hero.js creaturesAndItemsMap are untouched (zero symbol-crash risk). BALANCE GOTCHA: the gScore auto-equip weights every stat equally, so an armor/defense-heavy affix is picked over an equal-scored offensive one and quietly dilutes the hero's damage — an armor-tilted first draft dropped a 500-run sample to 24.4% (below the 25% floor). Reweighted the batch offense/HP-first (the stats that actually convert to boss-attrition wins) and it lands balance-neutral: isolated A/B (worktree off clean origin/main) measured baseline 29.1% vs batch 29.6% at 3000 runs each (+0.5pt, within noise), and the 6000-run --check gate passed at 27.6%; pooled ~28% over 9000 batch runs. luck (index 2) is inert in combat and gScore so it's 0 in every new affix (nonzero would pad).

Added a BIOME system to js/realm.js: every realm's rooms share one depth-themed architecture, mapped to mirror the enemy-family ladder (r1 halls, r2 frost 'Frostmarch', r3 blight 'Blightwood', r4+ ember 'Emberdeep' — biomeForRealm clamps like familyForRealm). The sprite sheet is a single 16-tile row with NO spare art (assets/sprite.png is 512x32; col15 is already the dead-hero sprite), so biomes can't have new floor/wall art — instead each biome RE-WEIGHTS the base bomberman offshoot rule via a new placeOffshoot(): halls = uniform (reproduces the original generator), frost = vertical-biased colonnades, blight = lateral-biased warren, ember = sparse sprout (0.55) open caverns. Reachable in play: makeRealm locks in the biome and every makeRoom uses it (verified in-browser via scene.reset()+levelUp() across realms 1-6 — all biomes render, zero console/thrown errors). spriteMap coverage: biomes only ever emit '#'/' ' (both already covered), so scene.js/hero.js are UNTOUCHED and there is zero symbol-crash risk. BALANCE: neutral BY CONSTRUCTION — selfplay's playthrough() abstracts the spatial layer and never calls makeRoom, so the win rate is unchanged (28.4% at 6000 runs vs 28.2% baseline, well inside [25,75]). The load-bearing NEW gate for this structural module is a room-connectivity + symbol validator added to scripts/selfplay.mjs (--rooms, and folded into --check): it flood-fills every generated room from the hero spawn [5,9] and asserts a single walkable component + only known symbols. CONNECTIVITY is preserved by construction — every pillar still sprouts AT MOST ONE wall and pass-2 walls never grow 'west' (keeps the wall-forest acyclic); placeOffshoot has a bounded retry + guaranteed-free escape cell so generation always terminates. GOTCHA for future room work: a naive denser biome that adds >1 wall/pillar or lets pass-2 sprout west CAN wall off a region — keep the ≤1-offshoot + no-west invariant, or the --rooms gate will (correctly) fail.

Data-driven rebalance pass. The win rate had drifted to the LOW band edge — selfplay --check read ~28% (only ~3pt over the 25% floor), a fragile margin. Per-realm diagnostics showed realms 5-7 own nearly every win and death, ~70%+ of losses are boss attrition, and HALF of all boss deaths landed with the boss already under 20% HP (median 19.7% left): the hero loses razor-thin, a hair short on damage/effective-HP. Chose the single most load-bearing, best-behaved lever: the global realm-scaling exponent in lj.enemy.base (js/enemy.js), realmMod = (1 + exp)^(size-1). A sweep was clean and monotonic (0.40->28%, 0.39->31.5%, 0.385->34%, 0.38->37%). Eased 0.40 -> 0.375, which the shipped `node scripts/selfplay.mjs --runs 5000 --check` measured at 37.2% (1859/5000) IN-BAND, beatable+survivable, structural gate green (26,400 rooms). Why the exponent over a hero buff: hero GEAR is unscaled (items default to itemLevel 1) while enemies scale with depth, so a flat hero-base buff mostly eases realm 1-3 (where ~0 deaths happen); the exponent's effect is inherently back-loaded to the deep realms where the game is actually decided, AND it eases the compounding armor/HP the tier comments call the depth villain — while preserving every tier's RELATIVE step (all families scale by the same factor). Restrained on purpose: 37% roughly quadruples the floor margin (3->12pt) yet keeps a sub-40% 'hard' roguelike, far from the 75% ceiling; not an overshoot to 50%. At size 1 the exponent is inert (x^0=1) so realm 1 is byte-identical. Also refreshed two now-stale '1.4^(size-1)' code comments to 1.375. Independent audit confirmed: single-point knob, no degenerate enemies (min boss HP stays >=15), no loot/render/structural coupling.

Added 'The Obsidian Warden' (red grade 'W'), the SECOND boss mechanic and a DEFENSIVE foil to the Cinderwyrm's enrage: a WARD that raises a barrier absorbing every 3rd LANDED hero blow. lj.enemy.monster carries {type:'ward',interval:3} copied through lj.enemy.base; the fight loop (js/combat.js) counts the hero's landed strikes (heroStrikes) and zeroes the damage when lj.enemy.warded(enemy,heroStrikes) is true (strikeCount % interval === 0). Changes the fight's SHAPE not its numbers: the hero must sink extra blows, dragging the duel out and handing the boss more swings -> boss-attrition (the dominant deep loss mode). Reachable: bossGradeForRealm now ALTERNATES the two apex identities by realm parity so a deep run faces BOTH — 'T' Cinderwyrm on odd realms 5/7/9, 'W' Warden on even 6/8/10 (both live only on red, the clamped realm-5+ family). Wired via isBoss('W'), hero.js creaturesAndItemsMap, scene.js spriteMap (reuses boss sprite [0,9]); selfplay fights the parity grade so both mechanics are measured. BALANCE: raw HP set LOW at 82 (ward wastes ~1/3 of hits -> ~123 effective, apex range) with FLAT [5,7] offense and untouched [5,2] armor/defense; the ward carries the threat. Load-bearing + Cinderwyrm-peer, both sim-measured: isolated A/B (all-deep = Warden) swings ~6-9pt ward off vs on; at HP 82 the isolated Warden reads ~39% vs the Cinderwyrm's ~38.5%, so the alternating shipped --check holds ~39% (baseline). Verified in a real browser: 400 Warden fights, every warded hit dealt 0, both outcomes, zero throws; all tile symbols incl 'W' paint without crashing; reachability 5->T 6->W 7->T 8->W confirmed.

Added the 'Trophies of the Arena' loot batch to js/stats.js: 4 gladiatorial prefixes (Gladiatorial, Bloodthirsty, Undaunted, Ironbound) + 4 arena-honour suffixes (of the Arena, of Conquest, of the Champion, of the Bulwark), themed to the HERO's own prowess (distinct from Spoils' enemy-family theme). Auto-reachable: makeItem draws rng across the full prefixes/suffixes arrays so pushing onto pre/suf is the only wiring; affixes never render as tiles (scene.js/hero.js untouched, zero symbol-crash risk, confirmed in a real browser + the --rooms structural gate). KEY FINDING — the carried-forward 'offense/HP-first, zero armor/def' loot heuristic is STALE post-rebalance and NO LONGER neutral: two different pure-offense drafts BOTH measured ~1.9pt LOW in a 10k-run isolated A/B (z~3.9 pooled), and an equipped-gear probe showed why — with the win rate now boss-attrition-bound (~72% of losses) and armor being %-mitigation that compounds at depth, the loot pool sits near an offense/defense SWEET SPOT, so pure-offense affixes out-gScore the hero's defensive items and STRIP ~4 pts of armor+defense off its boss gear (str/crit +5-6 but armor -1.5/def -2.6 => glass-cannon). Fix: COMPOSITION-MATCH the pool's own budget mix (~61% offense / ~24% hp / ~15% armor+defense) — stay offense/HP-LED (6 of 8 affixes) but let two defensive archetypes (Ironbound [1,0,0,1,0,0,2,3], of the Bulwark [0,0,0,3,0,0,1,3]) carry the ~15% armor/def share so the batch adds 'more of the same'. Result: balance-NEUTRAL — isolated 10k A/B baseline 37.76% vs batch 38.50% (+0.74pt, within noise; batch == pooled-baseline mean 38.50%), equipped defense restored to baseline; shipped `node scripts/selfplay.mjs --runs 6000 --check` read 38.5% (2312/6000) IN-BAND, structural green (26,400 rooms). of the Champion (gScore 10) ties of the Cinderwyrm as the suffix ceiling; luck (idx2) stays 0 (inert). GOTCHA for future loot runs: do NOT ship pure-offense affixes — MATCH the pool composition (~15% armor/def) or the win rate drifts ~2pt low.

Added 'The Searing Colossus' (red grade 'S'), the THIRD boss mechanic and a RETALIATION foil to enrage/ward: THORNS. Its molten hide sears back a fraction (0.20) of every blow the hero LANDS — lj.enemy.monster carries {type:'thorns',fraction} copied through lj.enemy.base, and a new helper lj.enemy.thorns(enemy,heroDamage) returns Math.round(heroDamage*fraction) (0 for non-thorns/whiffed). The fight loop (js/combat.js hero-turn branch) applies it AFTER the ward zeroing and ONLY when the boss survived the blow (`if (enemyHealth>0)`), folding the recoil into the hero's OWN log entry (lastAction.reflected) + heroHealth + heroDamageTaken. DISTINCTNESS: it is the deliberate OPPOSITE lever from the other two — enrage/ward make the boss survive LONGER (feeding boss-attrition); thorns never touches the boss's bar and never lengthens the duel, it converts the hero's own burst into self-damage, so the fast burst that beats the wyrm is what stings most while a patient armored hero is punished least. Self-scaling (recoil tracks the hero's hit, potent at any gear level). NOTE — a SIPHON (lifesteal) first draft was built then FULLY REVERTED: it proved a weak lever here (isolated A/B swing only ~2.6pt at HP78/frac0.33) because the hero's armor reduces boss damage — hence its lifesteal — to a trickle; thorns scales with the hero's LARGE damage instead. REQUIRED SUPPORT CHANGE: fight() outcome.actor switched from `lastAction.actor` to `heroHealth>0?'hero':'enemy'` — thorns can drop the hero on the hero's OWN turn, which lastAction.actor would miscredit as a win; because thorns is skipped on the killing blow, heroHealth<=0 always means a live boss finished the hero, so the health check is EXACTLY equivalent to lastAction.actor for base/enrage/ward and correct for thorns. showFightStat (js/hero.js) applies lastAction.reflected via hurt() during the hero's strike so the animated health tracks the outcome (harness applies outcome.damage once; neither double-counts). Reachable: bossGradeForRealm now rotates the THREE apexes on size%3 — T Cinderwyrm (5,8,11), W Warden (6,9,12), S Colossus (7,10,13), all red; wired via isBoss('S') + hero.js creaturesAndItemsMap.S + scene.js spriteMap.S (reuses boss sprite [0,9]); selfplay fights bossGradeForRealm so all three are auto-measured. BALANCE: PEER by construction — reuses the Warden's exact defensive block [5,7,82,1,3,5,2] (modest HP, mechanic carries the threat), thorns tuned to land the isolated all-Colossus rate dead level with the siblings. Measured: isolated A/B (all deep = Colossus, thorns off vs on) OFF 45.4% -> ON 38.4% at 4000 runs (-7.0pt swing, ~9 sigma => LOAD-BEARING); isolated ON 38.4% ~= Cinderwyrm ~38.5% / Warden ~39% (PEER); shipped `node scripts/selfplay.mjs --runs 6000 --check` read 37.7% (2262/6000) IN-BAND [25,75], beatable+survivable, structural green (26,400 rooms). Browser-verified on dist/: zero console errors, rotation T,W,S,T,W,S, a live realm-7 Colossus fight fired thorns 52x with the killing blow reflecting 0, and all grades (B/T/W/S/C/a/d) render crash-free. GOTCHA for a future 4th mechanic: thorns is the last 'shape' that keeps fights SHORT — a lifesteal/regen mechanic lengthens fights (bad at the 400ms/step animation) AND is weak against the hero's armor; prefer hero-damage-scaling or offense/tempo mechanics. The outcome.actor health-check is now the shared contract — a mechanic that can kill the hero on its OWN turn must skip the killing blow like thorns does, or re-derive attribution.

Added the 'Relics of the Apex' loot batch to js/stats.js: 4 prefixes (Furious, Barbed, Bastioned, Titanic) + 4 suffixes (of Fury, of Recoil, of the Aegis, of the Titan), themed to the three apex mechanics the deep hero faces (enrage=fury, ward=aegis, thorns=barbed/recoil) + an apex crown (Titan) — a fresh motif distinct from Spoils (enemy families) and Trophies (hero prowess). Auto-reachable: makeItem draws rng across the full prefixes/suffixes arrays so pushing onto pre/suf is the only wiring; affixes never render as tiles (scene.js/hero.js untouched, zero symbol-crash risk; browser-verified). KEY FINDING that EXTENDS the composition-match rule: composition-matching is NECESSARY BUT NOT SUFFICIENT. A first draft matching the pool's ~60/25/15 offense/hp/armor+def mix EXACTLY still eased the win rate +1.46pt (z=3.3 at 24k) — a real stealth-rebalance. An equipped-gear probe (realms 6-7) traced it to +3.7 inflated equipped HP; the ENABLER is that CRIT is combat-inert (~10% of hits) but FULL-WEIGHT in gScore, so crit+hp affixes over-equip and smuggle survivability HP. The neutral sibling Trophies avoids this (crit capped 8, hp paired with str/agi) and re-measures dead-neutral at 24k (0.00pt). FIX = copy that profile: crit total 8 (not 11), hp on str/agi or the defensive block (never crit-heavy), def>=armor, hp lean. Two defensive archetypes (Bastioned, of the Aegis) carry the ~15% armor/def share. Iterated v1(+1.46) -> v3 crit8(+0.88,z1.97) -> v4 hp-trimmed(+0.21pt, z=0.48 NEUTRAL). Totals str17/agi8/hp12/hit3/crit8/armor3/def6, comp 63/21/16, gScore max 8, luck+mf 0. Shipped --check 37.7% (2261/6000) IN-BAND, structural green (26,400 rooms). NEW GOTCHA: crit is a stealth power-adder — cap batch crit ~8 and don't co-locate crit+hp, or verify with an equipped-gear probe, not just composition math.

Add a FOURTH distinct boss mechanic and extend the apex rotation from size%3 to size%4 (T/W/S/new for realms >=5, all red). Follow the two-helper pattern EXACTLY (see enraged/warded/thorns): {type:...} on lj.enemy.monster through lj.enemy.base, a new small helper called from the js/combat.js fight loop, add the grade to isBoss + hero.js creaturesAndItemsMap + scene.js spriteMap (reuse boss sprite [0,9]); pick a FREE capital grade letter (avoid room-door letters D/U/L/R and existing grades a-d/B/T/W/S/C/E). GOTCHAS from boss-mechanic-3: (1) prefer a mechanic that keeps fights SHORT and scales off the HERO's damage or tempo — lifesteal/regen lengthens fights (bad at 400ms/step animation) and is weak vs the hero's high armor. (2) The fight's outcome.actor is now `heroHealth>0?'hero':'enemy'` (health-based); a mechanic that can kill the hero on the hero's OWN turn must skip the killing blow (as thorns does) so the health check stays valid, or re-derive attribution. GATE: isolated all-deep A/B ~ the siblings' ~38-39% (PEER) and load-bearing (mechanic on/off moves the isolated rate well beyond noise); shipped --check in-band; harness auto-measures bossGradeForRealm.
